calendar.matters:
AMENDED MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
Off Calendar Oct-05-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Christine Van Aken (415-554-4633) Deputy City Attorney for Defendant/opposing party City and County of San Francisco.
No other appearances.

PLAINTIFFs MARTIN COYNE, HOWARD WESTON, EDMUND CHUTE, and SMALL PROPERTY OWNERS OF SAN FRANCISCO INSTITUTE's AMENDED MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES is DENIED. The estimated value of this case exceeds by a substantial margin the actual litigation costs. Petitioners' probability of success on the facial challenge was at least 25% and the probability of success on as-applied challenge was at least 90%, i.e. a probability of success in this action was 57% with estimated discounted valued of the case of $748,669.35. Order is Signed in open court. (See Scanned Order for Details) Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported (D501)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Oct-02-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Andrew Zacks, Esq. and Justin Goodman, Esq. (415-956-8100) of Zacks & Freedman for Plaintiff/Petitioner/ Moving party Howard Weston, Martin Coyne, Edmund Chuite, Small Property Owners of San Francisco Institute.
Christine Van Aken, Deputy City Attorney (415-554-4633) for Defendant/Respondent/opposing party City and County of San Francisco.
Court reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis (415-948-8289) CSR #12998, Reported.

The Court announces it's Concerns on retroactivity and whether or not there are additional hurdles for landlord to overcome before it can avail itself of the Ellis Act right. (Campos 1 and Campos 2). Hearing is held. Both sides Argue. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Petition for Writ of Mandate is taken under submission (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis (415-948-8289) CSR #12998, Reported.(D501)
